Loops.Description: Table: Loops — build history and forward plan, carried as a first-class entity inside the model. Each row documents what new concept is introduced, what was witnessed, and (for planned rows) the next empirical question. Loops 62–67 are the corpus-scale research sweep + catalog expansion. Loops 68+ are the expansion encode waves: pre-register in DiscoveryHypotheses, encode via StudyImportTemplate, LoopReview at end of each turn before committing vocabulary.

schema.name: LoopId
schema.Description: Unique identifier: loop-01 through loop-10.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short title for what this loop adds to the domain model.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: planned | in-progress | complete

schema.name: NewConcept
schema.Description: The new first-class domain concept introduced this loop.

schema.name: DomainQuestion
schema.Description: The natural-language question this loop's concept answers FROM the domain data.

schema.name: MockDataNote
schema.Description: What the seed data is designed to show.

schema.name: NextSuggestion
schema.Description: Suggestion for the next loop, written after witnessing the DAG at this loop's end.

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose conceptual vocabulary this loop primarily draws on — tracks which tradition has driven each stage of the instrument's development.

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionCoreConern
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.CoreConcern via TraditionId — which intellectual concern drove this loop.

schema.name: CommitHash
schema.Description: Full git SHA where this loop row first landed in effortless-rulebook.json — the replay anchor for this turn of the Leopold loop.

schema.name: CommitShort
schema.Description: First 7 characters of CommitHash for display and git checkout.

schema.name: CommitDate
schema.Description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the commit where this loop landed.

schema.name: CommitMessage
schema.Description: Git commit subject line for this loop's landing commit.

schema.name: GitTag
schema.Description: Optional milestone tag pointing at this loop's landing commit (sp-loop-milestone-*). Used for discovery replay without memorizing SHAs.

Studies.Description: Table: Studies — a clinical or empirical study comparing two or more treatments for a condition, with patient cases stratified by a confounding variable. The top-level entity in the domain.

schema.name: StudyId
schema.Description: Unique slug for this study (e.g. kidney-1986, berkeley-1973).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label, mirrors StudyId.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Human-readable title of the study.

schema.name: Source
schema.Description: Citation or description of the original data source.

schema.name: SourceUrl
schema.Description: URL to the original source.

schema.name: PublicationYear
schema.Description: Year the study was published. Used for temporal stratification and pre-registration dating.

schema.name: Domain
schema.Description: Research domain: medicine | epidemiology | social-science | education | legal | sports | economics | synthetic. Enables cross-domain SignalPurity stratification.

schema.name: IsSynthetic
schema.Description: TRUE for constructed/counterfactual studies; FALSE for real published studies. Filters the corpus for empirical claims.

schema.name: TotalCases
schema.Description: Total number of cases (trials) across all CaseCells in this study.

schema.name: CellCount
schema.Description: Number of CaseCell rows for this study.

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose framing best characterizes this study's paradox type. Synthetic studies inherit the tradition of their conceptual origin.

schema.name: PrimaryResearcherId
schema.Description: FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher most associated with first surfacing or canonically citing this study in the Simpson's Paradox literature.

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionCoreConern
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.CoreConcern via TraditionId.

schema.name: PrimaryResearcherName
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Name via PrimaryResearcherId.

schema.name: PrimaryResearcherAffiliation
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Affiliation via PrimaryResearcherId.

schema.name: DistortionType
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.DistortionType for this study — the geometric classification of its paradox.

schema.name: PolicyImplication
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.CorrectedPolicyImplication for this study.

schema.name: AllocationDistortion
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.AllocationDistortion for this study — the scalar severity of allocation-induced distortion.

schema.name: SignalPurity
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.SignalPurity for this study — ratio of true signal to total gap width.

schema.name: IsSignFlip
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.IsSignFlip for this study — true if the paradox satisfies the continuous sign-flip definition.

schema.name: CorrectedWinner
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.CorrectedWinner for this study — which treatment wins after allocation correction.

schema.name: ConfoundingVariable
schema.Description: Lookup: StratumVariables.VariableName for this study — the named stratifying variable.

schema.name: CausalRole
schema.Description: Lookup: StratumVariables.CausalRole for this study — confounder, mediator, collider, proxy, or contested.

schema.name: IngestionCellParity
schema.Description: TRUE when CellCount = 2 x StratumCount.

schema.name: IngestionCompliance
schema.Description: all | partial | exempt-synthetic.

schema.name: IsControlStudy
schema.Description: TRUE if this study is a control (no Simpson's paradox expected/found); FALSE for the main Simpson's Paradox corpus.

schema.name: ControlDomain
schema.Description: Domain slug for control studies (e.g. medicine-rct, education-higher). NULL for non-control studies.

schema.name: ValidTimeStart
schema.Description: First year of the real-world cohort period this study's data covers (valid-time, not publication year). Defaults to PublicationYear when the true cohort window is not independently known.

schema.name: ValidTimeEnd
schema.Description: Last year of the real-world cohort period this study's data covers (valid-time, not publication year). Defaults to PublicationYear when the true cohort window is not independently known.

schema.name: ValidTimeMidpoint
schema.Description: Midpoint year of the valid-time cohort window, used to bucket studies by when their underlying data actually occurred rather than when the paper was published.

schema.name: ValidTimeDecade
schema.Description: ValidTimeMidpoint floored to the decade (e.g. 1973 -> 1970). The bucket key ConfounderDistortionTimeline aggregates over.

Treatments.Description: Table: Treatments — the interventions being compared within a study. Each treatment belongs to one study.

schema.name: TreatmentId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-A, kidney-1986-B).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Study
schema.Description: FK to the parent Study.

schema.name: TreatmentLabel
schema.Description: Short label used in CaseCells and comparisons (e.g. A, B).

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Plain-language description of what this treatment is.

schema.name: TotalCases
schema.Description: Total cases for this treatment across all strata.

schema.name: TotalSuccesses
schema.Description: Total successes for this treatment across all strata.

schema.name: PooledSuccessRate
schema.Description: Pooled (across all strata) success rate for this treatment: TotalSuccesses / TotalCases.

Strata.Description: Table: Strata — the subdivisions of patients within a study by the value of the confounding variable (e.g. stone size). Each stratum belongs to one study.

schema.name: StratumId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-small, kidney-1986-large).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Study
schema.Description: FK to the parent Study.

schema.name: StratumLabel
schema.Description: Short label used in CaseCells (e.g. small, large, dept-A).

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Plain-language description of what defines this stratum.

schema.name: TotalCases
schema.Description: Total cases in this stratum across all treatments.

CaseCells.Description: Table: CaseCells — the raw leaves of the DAG. One row per (study, stratum, treatment) combination recording the aggregate count of successes and total cases. Every derived fact in the model traces back to these numbers. Nothing above this row is a raw input.

schema.name: CaseCellId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-small-A).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Study
schema.Description: FK to the parent Study.

schema.name: StratumLabel
schema.Description: The stratum this cell belongs to (matches Strata.StratumLabel).

schema.name: TreatmentLabel
schema.Description: The treatment applied in this cell (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

schema.name: Successes
schema.Description: Number of patients in this cell who had a successful outcome. RAW INPUT — never derived.

schema.name: Cases
schema.Description: Total number of patients in this cell. RAW INPUT — never derived.

schema.name: CellSuccessRate
schema.Description: Success rate within this cell: Successes / Cases. The first derived fact in the DAG. Fixed loop-90: this rulebook's IF(x=0,"",...) idiom transpiles to a bare ''::numeric cast that errors at runtime for the one CaseCells row with Cases=0 (surfaced only once _erb_tr_metrics stopped masking it). Returns 0 for the degenerate zero-cases cell instead.

schema.name: TotalCasesForTreatment
schema.Description: Total cases across ALL strata for this treatment in this study — the denominator for TreatmentExposureFraction.

schema.name: TreatmentExposureFraction
schema.Description: Fraction of this treatment's total cases that fall in this stratum: Cases / TotalCasesForTreatment. High imbalance across strata is the mechanism of confounding — when treatments are allocated very differently across strata, the pooled rate conflates treatment effect with stratum difficulty.

schema.name: IsValidIngestionCell
schema.Description: TRUE when Cases>=0, Successes>=0, and Successes<=Cases (loop-92: a stratum-treatment combination can legitimately have zero observed cases in real source data -- e.g. rdatasets-mtcars-engine has zero 8-cylinder straight-engine cars -- so Cases=0 is a valid ingestion cell, not a defect).

StratumSummaries.Description: Table: StratumSummaries — one row per (study, stratum, treatment). Computes the success rate WITHIN each stratum for each treatment. This is the stratified view that contradicts the pooled conclusion when a Simpson's Paradox reversal is present. Each row belongs to both a Stratum and a Treatment in the same Study.

schema.name: StratumSummaryId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-small-A).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Study
schema.Description: FK to the parent Study.

schema.name: StratumLabel
schema.Description: Which stratum (matches Strata.StratumLabel).

schema.name: TreatmentLabel
schema.Description: Which treatment (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

schema.name: StratumSuccesses
schema.Description: Total successes in this (stratum, treatment) cell for this study.

schema.name: StratumCases
schema.Description: Total cases in this (stratum, treatment) cell for this study.

schema.name: StratumSuccessRate
schema.Description: Success rate within this stratum for this treatment: StratumSuccesses / StratumCases. The true per-stratum picture.

schema.name: StratumSuccessesA
schema.Description: Successes for treatment A in this (study, stratum) — used to determine the per-stratum winner.

schema.name: StratumCasesA
schema.Description: Cases for treatment A in this (study, stratum).

schema.name: StratumRateA
schema.Description: Success rate for treatment A in this stratum.

schema.name: StratumSuccessesB
schema.Description: Successes for treatment B in this (study, stratum).

schema.name: StratumCasesB
schema.Description: Cases for treatment B in this (study, stratum).

schema.name: StratumRateB
schema.Description: Success rate for treatment B in this stratum.

schema.name: StratumWinner
schema.Description: Which treatment wins in this stratum: 'A' if StratumRateA > StratumRateB, else 'B'. Used by TreatmentRankings to detect paradox.

schema.name: StudyTotalCases
schema.Description: Total cases across ALL strata and ALL treatments in this study. The denominator for StratumFraction.

schema.name: StratumTotalCases
schema.Description: Total cases in this stratum across both treatments (A + B combined). Numerator for StratumFraction.

schema.name: StratumFraction
schema.Description: Fraction of the study's total cases that fall in this stratum: StratumTotalCases / StudyTotalCases. The weight the confounder assigns to each stratum. A large StratumFraction on a low-success stratum pulls the pooled rate down; when that stratum is also over-allocated to one treatment, it creates the reversal.

schema.name: WeightedStratumRate
schema.Description: StratumFraction × StratumSuccessRate. The contribution of this (stratum, treatment) row to the study-level pooled rate. Summing WeightedStratumRate across all strata for a given treatment reconstructs that treatment's pooled rate from stratum weights — witnessing the mechanism equation.

schema.name: TreatmentACasesHere
schema.Description: Cases for treatment A in this stratum — used to compute TreatmentA's allocation fraction in this stratum.

schema.name: TreatmentBCasesHere
schema.Description: Cases for treatment B in this stratum — used to compute TreatmentB's allocation fraction in this stratum.

schema.name: TreatmentATotalCases
schema.Description: Total cases for treatment A across all strata in this study — denominator for AllocationFractionA.

schema.name: TreatmentBTotalCases
schema.Description: Total cases for treatment B across all strata in this study — denominator for AllocationFractionB.

schema.name: AllocationFractionA
schema.Description: Fraction of treatment A's total cases that land in this stratum: TreatmentACasesHere / TreatmentATotalCases. Equivalent to TreatmentExposureFraction for A.

schema.name: AllocationFractionB
schema.Description: Fraction of treatment B's total cases that land in this stratum: TreatmentBCasesHere / TreatmentBTotalCases.

schema.name: AllocationBias
schema.Description: AllocationFractionA minus AllocationFractionB: how much more of treatment A (relative to B) is concentrated in this stratum. Positive = A is over-allocated here; negative = B is over-allocated here. When AllocationBias is large and negative in a low-success stratum, treatment A is under-represented in the hard cases — making A look worse in the pooled view than it is in any stratum. This is the confounder's fingerprint as a signed number.

schema.name: StratumGap
schema.Description: Signed rate difference within this stratum: StratumRateA minus StratumRateB. Positive = A wins here; negative = B wins here; zero = tied. When every stratum has StratumGap > 0 but the pooled gap is negative, the contradiction IS Simpson's Paradox made arithmetically visible in one column. Multiply StratumGap × StratumFraction and sum across strata to get the equal-weight pooled gap (the signal allocation would produce without confounding).

schema.name: WeightedStratumGap
schema.Description: StratumGap × StratumFraction: the contribution of this stratum's treatment-rate difference to the equal-weight pooled gap. Summing WeightedStratumGap across all TreatmentA rows for a study gives WeightedStratumGapSum — the pooled gap the study would show under balanced allocation. This is the per-stratum building block of the sign-flip test.

schema.name: AbsAllocationBias
schema.Description: ABS(AllocationBias). MAXIFS-friendly unsigned magnitude so TreatmentRankings.MaxStratumImbalance can compute live via MAXIFS instead of a hand-written cache.

schema.name: AbsStratumGap
schema.Description: ABS(StratumGap). MAXIFS-friendly unsigned magnitude so TreatmentRankings.MaxStratumGap can compute live via MAXIFS instead of a hand-written cache.

ModelSummary.Description: Table: ModelSummary — one row describing the epistemic coverage of this entire model instance. Aggregates across all TreatmentRankings to witness how many studies were examined, how many showed a full reversal, how many were explained, and the average paradox strength. The model holds a mirror to itself.

schema.name: ModelSummaryId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this model instance.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ReversalCount
schema.Description: Number of studies with IsReversal=TRUE (sign-flip / allocation-driven reversal).

schema.name: NonReversalCount
schema.Description: Number of studies with IsReversal=FALSE.

schema.name: StudyCount
schema.Description: Total number of TreatmentRankings in this model: ReversalCount + NonReversalCount.

schema.name: ExplainedCount
schema.Description: Number of studies with IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (reversal AND confirmed confounder).

schema.name: TotalParadoxStrength
schema.Description: Sum of ParadoxStrength across all studies. Used to compute average.

schema.name: AvgParadoxStrength
schema.Description: Average ParadoxStrength across all studies: TotalParadoxStrength / StudyCount. A scalar summary of how paradox-rich this dataset is.

schema.name: TypeACount
schema.Description: Studies classified as Type-A: sign flip AND full reversal (ReversalIntensity=1). The canonical Simpson's Paradox — allocation distortion reverses the pooled winner.

schema.name: TypeBCount
schema.Description: Studies classified as Type-B: sign flip but partial reversal (ReversalIntensity<1). Allocation distortion creates a sign flip but the pooled signal partially reflects per-stratum reality.

schema.name: TypeDCount
schema.Description: Studies classified as Type-D: no sign flip, negligible distortion. Pooled analysis is trustworthy — allocation is sufficiently balanced.

schema.name: TypeAFraction
schema.Description: Fraction of studies that are Type-A (full canonical reversals). TypeACount / StudyCount.

schema.name: DistortionTaxonomyCoverage
schema.Description: Human-readable summary of the four-type distribution: e.g. 'A:2 B:1 C:1 D:0'. The model's self-portrait of its own distortion geometry.

schema.name: DistortionOnlyCount
schema.Description: Number of studies with real allocation distortion but no sign flip: TypeCCount. The pooled winner is correct but the effect size is biased. Distinct from ZeroStrengthCount, which conflates Type-C (real distortion) with Type-D (neutral).

schema.name: CAmplificationCount
schema.Description: Number of studies classified as type C+ (allocation-amplified): pooled winner is correct but pooled gap overstates true advantage. Causes researcher overconfidence — may accept higher cost/risk than true effect warrants.

schema.name: CCompressionCount
schema.Description: Number of studies classified as type C- (allocation-compressed): pooled winner is correct but pooled gap understates true advantage. Causes researcher underconfidence — effective treatment may be prematurely abandoned.

schema.name: AvgSignalPurity
schema.Description: Average SignalPurity across all TreatmentRankings. Below 0.5 means allocation noise exceeds true signal on average — the corpus is predominantly misleading at the pooled level. Above 0.5 means the corpus is predominantly trustworthy. Fixed loop-90: COUNTIFS(...,"<>")/SUMIFS(...,"<>") mistranslate to a literal '<>' string-equality filter (a known transpiler bug), always returning 0/NULL; replaced with SUM/COUNT, both of which transpile correctly.

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGapMax
schema.Description: Maximum SweepCorrectedGap across all AllocationSweep rows. Should equal SweepCorrectedGapMin — the invariant.

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGapMin
schema.Description: Minimum SweepCorrectedGap across all AllocationSweep rows. Should equal SweepCorrectedGapMax — the invariant.

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGapRange
schema.Description: SweepCorrectedGapMax − SweepCorrectedGapMin. The allocation-invariance witness: this should be zero (up to floating-point rounding). If nonzero, the corrected gap is not allocation-free.

schema.name: SweepPooledGapRange
schema.Description: MAX(SweepPooledGap) − MIN(SweepPooledGap) across all AllocationSweep rows. Expected to be large (≈0.21) — the pooled gap wanders as allocation shifts.

schema.name: RealStudyCount
schema.Description: Number of real (non-synthetic) published studies in the corpus. Retyped loop-90: was mis-declared raw with a formula, which is why the transpiler emitted no native function and 02b had to hand-write calc_model_summary_real_study_count.

schema.name: AvgSignalPurityReversal
schema.Description: Average SignalPurity across reversal studies (type A and B). Theorem predicts this must be < 0.5. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount); rewritten from SUMIFS/COUNTIFS division to AVERAGEIFS because the SUMIFS/COUNTIFS division form transpiled with a mangled cross-table alias.

schema.name: AvgSignalPurityNonReversal
schema.Description: Average SignalPurity across non-reversal studies (type C+, C-, D). Theorem predicts this must be >= 0.5 except edge cases. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount); rewritten from SUMIFS/COUNTIFS division to AVERAGEIFS (see AvgSignalPurityReversal).

schema.name: SignalPurityGap
schema.Description: Separation between reversal and non-reversal average SignalPurity. The larger this gap, the more discriminating the threshold at 0.5. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

schema.name: MedicineStudyCount
schema.Description: Real published studies in the medicine domain. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

schema.name: EpidemiologyStudyCount
schema.Description: Real published studies in the epidemiology domain. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

schema.name: OtherDomainStudyCount
schema.Description: Real published studies outside medicine and epidemiology (sports, legal, education, social-science). Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount).

schema.name: DomainDiversityNote
schema.formula: =CONCAT("real=", {{RealStudyCount}}, " studies; wave2=", {{ExpansionWave2StudyCount}}, "; eduLatent=", ROUND({{EducationLatentFraction}} * 100, 0), "%; sportsLatent=", ROUND({{SportsLatentFraction}} * 100, 0), "%; econFlipRate=", ROUND({{EconomicsSignFlipRate}} * 100, 0), "%")
schema.Description: Human-readable domain breakdown. Witnesses corpus diversity — a prerequisite for cross-domain SignalPurity claims. Retyped loop-90 (see RealStudyCount); TEXT(...,"0%") is not a supported transpiler function, replaced with ROUND(x*100,0) + literal '%'.

schema.name: SyntheticPhaseCount
schema.Description: Number of parameter grid points in SyntheticPhase. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: PhaseDiagramComplete
schema.Description: TRUE when all five DistortionType cells are populated in the synthetic phase grid. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b.

schema.name: PhaseTaxonomyCoverage
schema.Description: Type counts across the synthetic phase grid. Refreshed from vw_phase_diagram_summary by 05b.

schema.name: IngestionProtocolItemCount
schema.Description: Number of rows in IngestionProtocol — the documented contract items. Fixed loop-90 (see IngestionSummary.ProtocolItemCount for why COUNTIFS(...,"<>") is unsafe).

schema.name: CorpusPassesIngestionContract
schema.Description: Lookup: IngestionSummary.IngestionContractPasses.

schema.name: IngestionWitnessNote
schema.Description: Human-readable ingestion contract witness from IngestionSummary.

schema.name: CatalogEntryCount
schema.Description: Total catalog rows.

schema.name: PendingImportCount
schema.Description: Candidate studies queued.

schema.name: ReadyToEncodeCount
schema.Description: Encode-ready candidates.

schema.name: ImportSessionReady
schema.Description: Bulk import session readiness flag.

schema.name: CatalogWitnessNote
schema.Description: Import backlog witness string.

schema.name: LatentTypeDCount
schema.Description: Type-D studies with LatentFlipPotential=TRUE.

schema.name: StableTypeDCount
schema.Description: Type-D studies without latent flip potential (allocation-stable at observed and counterfactual sweep).

schema.name: LatentTypeDFraction
schema.Description: Fraction of Type-D studies with latent flip potential.

schema.name: CrossZeroCount
schema.Description: Studies whose pooled gap crosses zero under allocation sweep.

schema.name: SignFlipSignalPurityMax
schema.Description: Maximum SignalPurity among sign-flip studies. Must stay below 0.5 for the signal-purity theorem.

schema.name: EconomicsSignFlipCount
schema.Description: Sign-flip count in economics domain.

schema.name: SumPooledGapLatentD
schema.Description: SUM(PooledGap) among Type-D studies with latent flip potential — a bare, unwrapped SUMIFS split out as its own field because nesting a cross-table SUMIFS/AVERAGEIFS as an operand inside another IF/division expression transpiles with a mangled WHERE-clause column alias (e.g. rankings_latent_flip_potential) that errors at runtime; a bare top-level SUMIFS transpiles correctly (loop-90).

schema.name: AvgPooledGapLatentD
schema.Description: Average PooledGap among Type-D studies with latent flip potential. Fixed loop-90 (see SumPooledGapLatentD for why the sum is split into its own field).

schema.name: SumPooledGapStableD
schema.Description: SUM(PooledGap) among allocation-stable Type-D studies — split out as a bare field for the same reason as SumPooledGapLatentD.

schema.name: AvgPooledGapStableD
schema.Description: Average PooledGap among allocation-stable Type-D studies. Fixed loop-90 (see SumPooledGapLatentD).

schema.name: EpidemiologyAvgDistortion
schema.Description: Mean AllocationDistortion in epidemiology studies.

schema.name: EducationAvgDistortion
schema.Description: Mean AllocationDistortion in education studies.

schema.name: ConfounderSignFlipCount
schema.Description: Sign-flip count among confounder-labeled studies.

schema.name: ConfounderLatentOnlyCount
schema.Description: Latent-only flip count among confounder-labeled studies.

schema.name: ColliderSelectionCount
schema.Description: Studies tagged collider or selection on StratumVariables.

schema.name: ColliderSelectionManifestCount
schema.Description: Manifest sign-flips among collider/selection studies.

schema.name: ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount
schema.Description: Latent-only flips among collider/selection studies.

schema.name: ExplainedConfounderCount
schema.Description: Count of TreatmentRankings rows where IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (should equal ConfounderSignFlipCount).

schema.name: ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount
schema.Description: Count of contested or mediator sign-flip rows where IsParadoxExplained=TRUE (should be zero).

schema.name: DiscoveryWitnessNote
schema.Description: Research sweep synthesis rollup (loops 61–66).

schema.name: TypePredictionMatchCount
schema.Description: Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMatchCount.

schema.name: TypePredictionMismatchCount
schema.Description: Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMismatchCount.

schema.name: TypePredictionMatchRate
schema.Description: Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.TypePredictionMatchRate.

schema.name: SignFlipPredictionMatchRate
schema.Description: Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.SignFlipPredictionMatchRate.

schema.name: CatalogPredictionWitnessNote
schema.Description: Lookup: CorpusCatalogSummary.CatalogPredictionWitnessNote.

schema.name: HighImbalanceSignFlipThreshold
schema.Description: max_stratum_imbalance cutoff for geometry-controlled domain screening (loop-66). Set from corpus median by customize SQL.

schema.name: EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount
schema.Description: Sign-flip count among economics studies with max_stratum_imbalance >= threshold.

schema.name: EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate
schema.Description: Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance epidemiology studies.

schema.name: LegalHighImbalanceSignFlipRate
schema.Description: Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance legal studies.

schema.name: SportsHighImbalanceSignFlipRate
schema.Description: Sign-flip rate among high-imbalance sports studies.

schema.name: CPlusAvgDistortion
schema.Description: Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-C+ studies.

schema.name: CMinusAvgDistortion
schema.Description: Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-C- studies.

schema.name: TypeDAvgDistortion
schema.Description: Mean AllocationDistortion among Type-D studies.

schema.name: SweepFragileCount
schema.Description: Count of TreatmentRankings with IsSweepFragile=TRUE.

schema.name: ExpansionWave1EconomicsExpectedACount
schema.Description: Imported expansion-wave-1 economics catalog rows tagged Expected A.

schema.name: ExpansionWave1EconomicsExpectedADCount
schema.Description: Expansion-wave-1 economics Expected-A imports observed as Type D.

schema.name: EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate
schema.Description: Fraction of expansion-wave-1 economics Expected-A imports observed as Type D.

schema.name: UnanimousSignFlipCount
schema.Description: Sign-flip studies with unanimous stratum direction (IsSignFlip AND IsStratumUnanimous).

schema.name: MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange
schema.Description: MAX(SweepStudySummary.SweepCorrectedGapRange) — must stay below 0.0001 for CorrectedGap invariance theorem.

schema.name: CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount
schema.Description: Count of SweepStudySummary rows where SweepCorrectedGapRange >= 0.0001.

schema.name: FalsePositiveExplainedCount
schema.Description: IsParadoxExplained=TRUE rows that are not confounder manifest sign-flips — must be 0 for explained↔confounder theorem.
schema.formula: =COUNTIFS(TreatmentRankings!{{IsParadoxExplained}}, TRUE(), TreatmentRankings!{{IsSignFlip}}, FALSE()) + COUNTIFS(TreatmentRankings!{{IsParadoxExplained}}, TRUE(), TreatmentRankings!{{IsSignFlip}}, TRUE(), TreatmentRankings!{{StratumCausalRole}}, "<>confounder")

schema.name: UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount
schema.Description: Confounder sign-flips with IsParadoxExplained=FALSE — must be 0 for explained↔confounder theorem.

schema.name: TheoremCount
schema.Description: Count of witnessed Conclusions with Category=corpus-theorem (portfolio rollup for loop-76; category renamed from theorem in loop-91).

schema.name: EducationLatentFraction
