StratumVariables.Description: Table: StratumVariables — one row per study, naming the variable that defines the strata and describing its causal role. A confounder affects both treatment assignment AND outcome; a mediator is downstream of treatment; a contested variable is one where the causal role is disputed. IsConfounder is derived from these two boolean flags.

schema.name: StratumVariableId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-stone-size).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Study
schema.Description: FK to the parent Study.

schema.name: VariableName
schema.Description: The name of the stratifying variable (e.g. stone_size, department, age_group).

schema.name: CausalRole
schema.Description: The variable's causal role: 'confounder' (affects both assignment and outcome), 'mediator' (downstream of treatment), 'contested' (causal role disputed), 'unknown'.

schema.name: AffectsTreatmentAssignment
schema.Description: TRUE if this variable causally influences which treatment a patient receives. Required for confounding.

schema.name: AffectsOutcome
schema.Description: TRUE if this variable causally influences the outcome independently of treatment. Required for confounding.

schema.name: IsConfounder
schema.Description: TRUE when AffectsTreatmentAssignment AND AffectsOutcome AND CausalRole = 'confounder'. A confounder is the classic driver of Simpson's Paradox.

schema.name: MechanismNote
schema.Description: Plain-language description of how this variable creates the confounding: why it affects both assignment and outcome.

schema.name: ConditioningRisk
schema.Description: The causal hazard posed by conditioning on this variable. Values: confounder (safe to condition — removes bias), mediator (conditioning blocks the causal path of interest — harmful), collider (conditioning opens a spurious backdoor — harmful), proxy (conditioning on a noisy proxy of the true confounder — risky), none (no conditioning risk identified). NULL means unclassified.

schema.name: ConfounderIdentity
schema.Description: Canonical cross-study identity via StratumVariableIdentityMaps (loop-80).

TreatmentRankings.Description: Table: TreatmentRankings — one row per treatment-pair comparison within a study. Derives the pooled winner, the per-stratum winner, and the IsReversal boolean. The paradox emerges here as a derived fact: IsReversal is true when the pooled winner and the per-stratum winner disagree.

schema.name: TreatmentRankingId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. kidney-1986-A-vs-B).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Study
schema.Description: FK to the parent Study.

schema.name: TreatmentA
schema.Description: Label for the first treatment in the comparison (matches Treatments.TreatmentLabel).

schema.name: TreatmentB
schema.Description: Label for the second treatment in the comparison.

schema.name: TotalCasesA
schema.Description: Total cases for TreatmentA across all strata in this study.

schema.name: TotalSuccessesA
schema.Description: Total successes for TreatmentA across all strata.

schema.name: PooledRateA
schema.Description: Pooled success rate for TreatmentA: TotalSuccessesA / TotalCasesA.

schema.name: TotalCasesB
schema.Description: Total cases for TreatmentB across all strata.

schema.name: TotalSuccessesB
schema.Description: Total successes for TreatmentB across all strata.

schema.name: PooledRateB
schema.Description: Pooled success rate for TreatmentB: TotalSuccessesB / TotalCasesB.

schema.name: PooledWinner
schema.Description: Which treatment wins when strata are ignored. Returns tie when pooled rates are equal within floating-point tolerance.

schema.name: StratumCount
schema.Description: Number of strata in this study. Counted from StratumSummaries filtered to TreatmentA rows (one row per stratum).

schema.name: StrataWonByA
schema.Description: Number of strata where A wins: count StratumSummaries rows whose TreatmentLabel=TreatmentA AND StratumWinner=TreatmentA. These are the one-per-stratum sentinel rows.

schema.name: StrataWonByB
schema.Description: Number of strata where B wins: count StratumSummaries rows whose TreatmentLabel=TreatmentA AND StratumWinner=TreatmentB.

schema.name: PerStratumWinner
schema.Description: The treatment that wins in every stratum — 'A' if StrataWonByA = StratumCount, 'B' if StrataWonByB = StratumCount, else 'none' (no unanimous per-stratum winner).

schema.name: IsStratumUnanimous
schema.Description: TRUE when one treatment wins every stratum (StrataWonByA = StratumCount OR StrataWonByB = StratumCount). Maps A/B policy split: unanimous → stratify-immediately geometry; heterogeneous → investigate-confounder.

schema.name: IsReversal
schema.Description: TRUE when the equal-weight pooled signal (WeightedStratumGapSum) and the actual pooled signal (SignedPooledGap) point in opposite directions — allocation has flipped the direction of the aggregate conclusion. This is Simpson's Paradox as a derived fact.

schema.name: ConfoundersInStudy
schema.Description: Count of StratumVariables in this study whose IsConfounder = TRUE. When > 0, the paradox has a causal explanation.

schema.name: IsParadoxExplained
schema.Description: TRUE when IsReversal is present AND at least one confirmed confounder exists in the study.

schema.name: PooledGap
schema.Description: Absolute difference between the two pooled rates: |PooledRateA - PooledRateB|. The size of the aggregate misleading signal.

schema.name: StrataWonByLoser
schema.Description: Number of strata won by the pooled loser — the counter-signal. For full reversals this equals StratumCount; for partial paradoxes it is between 0 and StratumCount.

schema.name: ParadoxStrength
schema.Description: Scalar severity of the paradox: PooledGap × (StrataWonByLoser / StratumCount). Zero when no strata go against the pooled winner. Positive for partial paradoxes. Maximum when every stratum contradicts the pooled result.

schema.name: PooledRateFromWeightsA
schema.Description: Pooled rate for TreatmentA reconstructed as a weighted average of stratum rates: SUM(WeightedStratumRate) across all stratum rows for TreatmentA. Must match PooledRateA — this witnesses the mechanism equation: the pooled rate IS a weighted average of stratum rates, weighted by StratumFraction.

schema.name: PooledRateFromWeightsB
schema.Description: Pooled rate for TreatmentB reconstructed as a weighted average. Must match PooledRateB. If it does, the mechanism equation is verified: reversal is purely a consequence of differential weighting.

schema.name: ReversalIntensity
schema.Description: StrataWonByLoser / StratumCount: the fraction of strata that go against the pooled winner. Zero for no paradox, 1.0 for full reversal, between 0 and 1 for partial. This is the allocation-side measure of how deeply the confounding has penetrated the stratified view.

schema.name: ThresholdMargin
schema.Description: ReversalIntensity minus 0.5: positive when more than half the strata go against the pooled winner (reversal is robust), zero at the tipping point (exactly half), negative when fewer than half oppose the pooled winner (reversal is fragile or absent). A study with ThresholdMargin > 0 is robustly paradoxical; one with ThresholdMargin < 0 has a weak or absent paradox.

schema.name: SignedPooledGap
schema.Description: Signed difference between pooled rates: PooledRateA minus PooledRateB. Positive = A wins pooled; negative = B wins pooled. Unlike PooledGap (which is absolute), this preserves direction — essential for detecting sign flips between the equal-weight and actual pooled signals.

schema.name: WeightedStratumGapSum
schema.Description: Sum of WeightedStratumGap (= StratumGap × StratumFraction) across all StratumSummaries rows where TreatmentLabel = TreatmentA for this study. This is the equal-weight pooled gap — the gap the pooled analysis would produce if allocation were perfectly balanced across strata. When this has the opposite sign to SignedPooledGap, the allocation has not merely compressed the signal: it has flipped it entirely.

schema.name: IsSignFlip
schema.Description: TRUE when WeightedStratumGapSum and SignedPooledGap have opposite signs. Same condition as IsReversal; retained as the coordinate-level name in the DAG.

schema.name: AllocationDistortion
schema.Description: The magnitude of how far the allocation has bent the pooled signal: |WeightedStratumGapSum − SignedPooledGap|. Zero means the allocation is neutral — the pooled analysis faithfully represents the equal-weight stratum evidence. A large AllocationDistortion means the allocation is doing most of the work: the pooled number is mostly noise from how cases were assigned, not signal about which treatment is better. This measure does NOT require a sign flip to be nonzero — it captures any allocation-induced distortion, not just reversals.

schema.name: DistortionType
schema.Description: Geometric classification (five types A/B/C+/C-/D). Sign-flip types: A = unanimous stratum direction (IsStratumUnanimous); B = heterogeneous stratum directions. Non-flip: C+/C-/D by distortion ratio.
schema.formula: =IF({{AllocationDistortion}} = "", "", IF({{IsSignFlip}}, IF({{IsStratumUnanimous}}, "A", "B"), IF(AND(NOT({{IsSignFlip}}), {{AllocationDistortion}} > 0.01, ABS({{SignedPooledGap}}) > ABS({{CorrectedGap}}) + 0.001), "C+", IF(AND(NOT({{IsSignFlip}}), {{AllocationDistortion}} > 0.01, ABS({{SignedPooledGap}}) < ABS({{CorrectedGap}}) - 0.001), "C-", "D"))))

schema.name: PolicyImplication
schema.Description: The researcher action implied by the geometric classification. Derived from DistortionType: type A (full reversal) → 'stratify-immediately' — the pooled conclusion is directionally wrong; do not act on it without stratifying. Type B (partial sign-flip) → 'investigate-confounder' — the equal-weight signal opposes the pooled signal, but the per-stratum picture is mixed; the causal mechanism needs examination before trusting either estimate. Type C (compression without flip) → 'check-allocation-bias' — the pooled winner is probably correct but the effect size is distorted; report adjusted or standardized margins rather than the raw pooled gap. Type D (neutral) → 'pooled-analysis-trustworthy' — allocation is not materially distorting the signal; the pooled conclusion is safe with respect to this stratification.

schema.name: CorrectedGap
schema.Description: The allocation-corrected treatment gap: WeightedStratumGapSum, i.e. what SignedPooledGap would be if allocation were equal across strata. Positive means A is favoured in the corrected world; negative means B is favoured.

schema.name: CorrectedWinner
schema.Description: Which treatment wins once allocation bias is removed. Derived from CorrectedGap: positive → TreatmentA; negative → TreatmentB; near-zero → tie.

schema.name: CorrectedVsPooledAgreement
schema.Description: TRUE when the allocation-corrected winner matches the pooled winner. FALSE when removing allocation bias would change which treatment appears to win — the machine-readable definition of a reversal recovery.

schema.name: CorrectedPolicyImplication
schema.Description: The allocation-aware researcher action: what to do when the corrected verdict is available. Derived from DistortionType and CorrectedWinner together, making the instrument self-consistent with Reversal Recovery (loop-27). Type A (full sign-flip, unanimous per-stratum reversal): CorrectedWinner is the true signal — 'use-corrected-winner'. The pooled analysis was directionally wrong; act on CorrectedWinner, not PooledWinner. Type B (partial sign-flip, non-unanimous): CorrectedWinner points against the pooled signal but strata disagree among themselves — 'use-corrected-winner-with-caution'. Trust the corrected direction but acknowledge residual uncertainty. Type C (compression, no sign flip): PooledWinner is directionally correct; the distortion is in the magnitude, not the direction — 'check-allocation-bias'. Same as PolicyImplication. Type D (neutral): allocation is not materially distorting the signal — 'pooled-analysis-trustworthy'. Same as PolicyImplication. For Types C and D, CorrectedPolicyImplication and PolicyImplication always agree; for Types A and B, CorrectedPolicyImplication supersedes PolicyImplication with the allocation-corrected verdict.

schema.name: AllocationDirection
schema.Description: Whether the allocation is reversing, amplifying, or compressing the true treatment signal (CorrectedGap). 'reversal': allocation changed the sign of the pooled gap relative to CorrectedGap — worst case; pooled winner is wrong (types A and B). 'amplification': allocation inflated the pooled gap beyond CorrectedGap in the same direction — pooled winner is correct but effect size is overstated (type C+). 'compression': allocation shrank the pooled gap below CorrectedGap in the same direction — pooled winner is correct but effect size is understated (type C-). 'neutral': allocation had negligible effect on the pooled gap (type D). Policy consequences: reversal → wrong decision; amplification → overconfidence; compression → underconfidence / premature abandonment; neutral → trustworthy.

schema.name: SignalPurity
schema.Description: The fraction of total observed variation (|CorrectedGap| + AllocationDistortion) that is genuine treatment signal rather than allocation noise. SignalPurity = |CorrectedGap| / (|CorrectedGap| + AllocationDistortion). Range [0,1]: 1.0 = allocation contributed nothing (type D); 0 = corrected gap is zero (pure noise). Critical threshold is 0.5: SignalPurity < 0.5 means allocation noise exceeds true signal. The signal-purity theorem (loop-44): AllocationDirection='reversal' → SignalPurity < 0.5. Equivalently: a sign flip requires the confound to produce more distortion than the treatment produced signal. This is the mechanistic account of why reversals happen: not study-design accidents but algebraic necessities.

schema.name: PooledGapCrossesZero
schema.Description: Lookup: SweepStudySummary.PooledGapCrossesZero via Study. TRUE when reweighting allocation would change the sign of the pooled gap.

schema.name: SweepPooledGapRange
schema.Description: Lookup: SweepStudySummary.SweepPooledGapRange via Study. Width of pooled-gap wander under allocation sweep.

schema.name: LatentFlipPotential
schema.Description: TRUE when DistortionType=D but PooledGapCrossesZero=TRUE — SAFE at observed allocation but flip-capable under counterfactual reweighting.

schema.name: AllocationFragility
schema.Description: SweepPooledGapRange divided by |PooledGap|. High values mean small observed effect but wide counterfactual wander. Fixed loop-90: this rulebook's IF(x="","",...) blank-on-degenerate idiom transpiles to a bare ''::numeric cast that errors at runtime the moment a live row genuinely has PooledGap=0 (surfaced only once _erb_tr_metrics, which masked this row, was eliminated). Returns 0 for the degenerate case instead — see TreatmentRankings.DistortionRatio for the same fix pattern.

schema.name: MaxStratumImbalance
schema.Description: MAXIFS(StratumSummaries.AbsAllocationBias) over rows where Study matches and TreatmentLabel='A' — the largest allocation-share skew any single stratum shows for this study. Live aggregation; replaces the former hand-written _erb_tr_metrics cache column of the same name.

schema.name: MaxStratumGap
schema.Description: MAXIFS(StratumSummaries.AbsStratumGap) over rows where Study matches and TreatmentLabel='A' — the largest within-stratum rate gap for this study. Live aggregation; replaces the former hand-written _erb_tr_metrics cache column of the same name.

schema.name: IsSweepFragile
schema.Description: Type-D study that is pooled-safe but ultra-fragile under allocation reweighting: signal_purity=1, allocation_distortion=0, sweep_pooled_gap_range>0.3, allocation_fragility>=10.

schema.name: StudyDomain
schema.Description: Lookup: Studies.Domain via Study.

schema.name: StratumCausalRole
schema.Description: Lookup: StratumVariables.CausalRole for this study — confounder, mediator, collider, selection, contested, etc.

schema.name: IsLatentOnlyFlip
schema.Description: TRUE when allocation sweep crosses zero but observed allocation shows no sign flip — latent-only reversal.

schema.name: ConfirmedCausalRoleCount
schema.Description: Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is "confounder" — includes variables that confound the outcome regardless of whether allocation was balanced.

schema.name: MediatorRiskCount
schema.Description: Count of StratumVariables in this study whose ConditioningRisk is mediator or collider — the hazardous conditioning types.

schema.name: ContestedStratumCount
schema.Description: Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is contested — the causal interpretation is disputed.

schema.name: UnknownCausalRoleCount
schema.Description: Count of StratumVariables in this study whose CausalRole is unknown — no causal account has been established.

schema.name: CausalClaimStatus
schema.Description: The epistemic status of the causal interpretation for this study. Values: established (all stratum variables are confirmed confounders with known mechanisms), contested (at least one stratum variable has a disputed causal role — e.g. Berkeley 1973 where department choice may be endogenous), geometric-only (no stratum variable has an established causal role — the distortion is a geometric fact, not a causal claim). Derived from StratumVariables.CausalRole and ConditioningRisk.

schema.name: AdjustmentAppropriate
schema.Description: TRUE only when it is epistemically safe to act on CorrectedGap as the causal treatment effect estimate: no mediator/collider conditioning risk AND causal claim is established (not contested or geometric-only). FALSE for the birth-weight paradox (mediator), berkeley-1973 (contested), and synthetic/degenerate studies (geometric-only).

schema.name: AdjustmentRationale
schema.Description: Human-authored note explaining why AdjustmentAppropriate is TRUE or FALSE for this study. Covers mediator/collider hazards, contested causal roles, and cases where geometric correction is valid but causal interpretation is uncertain.

schema.name: PurityTrapFlag
schema.Description: TRUE when SignalPurity >= 0.90 AND the study's confounder identity has PolicyDefault IN ('do-not-condition','do-not-naively-adjust'). Marks the purity trap: data appears cleanest where naive stratification is most harmful.

schema.name: DistortionRatio
schema.Description: SignedPooledGap / WeightedStratumGapSum. Promoted loop-90 from a 03b-customize-views.sql presentation-layer bolt-on to a first-class rulebook field. The IF(x=0,"",...) blank-on-zero-denominator idiom used elsewhere in this rulebook is unsafe here: NULLIF is not a supported transpiler function, and the transpiler's translation of the "" branch for a numeric-typed field is a bare ''::numeric cast that errors at runtime the moment a live row actually hits it (4 of 242 TreatmentRankings rows have WeightedStratumGapSum=0). Returns 0 in that case instead — WeightedStratumGapSum=0 means there is no equal-weight baseline signal to compare against, so a 0 ratio (no distortion multiplier) is the defensible convention, not a guess.

schema.name: ScreeningTier
schema.Description: DANGER when DistortionType is A or B, CAUTION when C+/C-, SAFE when D. Promoted loop-90 (see DistortionRatio).

schema.name: ArmSizeRatio
schema.Description: TotalCasesA / (TotalCasesA + TotalCasesB) — the share of total cases allocated to TreatmentA. Promoted loop-90 (see DistortionRatio for why the empty-string zero-denominator idiom is unsafe on a numeric field). Returns 0 for the degenerate zero-case-count edge (never occurs in this corpus, kept for defensive symmetry with DistortionRatio).

schema.name: SymmetryDeparture
schema.Description: ABS(ArmSizeRatio - 0.5) — how far the case allocation departs from a balanced 50/50 split. Promoted loop-90 (see DistortionRatio).

InvariantChecks.Description: Table: InvariantChecks — one row per algebraic invariant across the 40-study validation corpus. PassCount and FailCount are populated by run_invariant_checks() in 05b-customize-data.sql. Any critical FailCount > 0 breaks the build.

schema.name: InvariantCheckId
schema.Description: Unique slug for this invariant (e.g. inv-balanced-alloc).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: AlgebraicStatement
schema.Description: The mathematical assertion in compact symbolic form.

schema.name: NaturalLanguage
schema.Description: Plain-English statement of what property must hold and why it is an invariant of the model.

schema.name: SourceTable
schema.Description: The DAG table whose rows are checked (TreatmentRankings or StratumSummaries).

schema.name: FilterExpression
schema.Description: Condition selecting the universe of rows this invariant applies to. Empty means all rows.

schema.name: AssertionExpression
schema.Description: The per-row condition that must be TRUE for every row in scope.

schema.name: SqlFilter
schema.Description: Optional SQL WHERE clause that selects the universe of rows in scope for this invariant (e.g. distortion_type = 'D'). Empty string means all rows.

schema.name: SqlAssertion
schema.Description: The SQL WHERE clause (without WHERE keyword) that counts PASSING rows from vw_treatment_rankings for this invariant. Used by run_invariant_checks() to evaluate each assertion dynamically from the rulebook table.

schema.name: PassCount
schema.Description: Rows where the invariant holds. Populated by 05b-customize-data.sql from a live query against vw_treatment_rankings.

schema.name: FailCount
schema.Description: Rows where the invariant is violated. Any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.

schema.name: UniverseCount
schema.Description: Total rows in scope (PassCount + FailCount).

schema.name: IsGreen
schema.Description: TRUE when FailCount = 0.

schema.name: StatusLabel
schema.Description: PASS or FAIL(n) summary for display.

schema.name: Severity
schema.Description: critical | warning — critical means any FailCount > 0 is a build-breaking bug.

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The tradition whose theoretical commitments this algebraic invariant protects — a failed invariant would falsify that tradition's key claim.

schema.name: ProtectsConclusion
schema.Description: FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion whose witness depends on this invariant holding. If this invariant fails, that conclusion is undermined.

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionKeyClaimSummary
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.KeyClaim via TraditionId — a failed invariant would falsify this tradition's key claim.

schema.name: ProtectsConclusionTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Title via ProtectsConclusion.

schema.name: ProtectsConclusionCategory
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Category via ProtectsConclusion.

MaterializedEntities.Description: Table: MaterializedEntities — governance record of every entity in this rulebook whose fields are principled materializations rather than live-calculated (per project CLAUDE.md: allowed only when the computation is genuinely expensive AND its upstream is sealed/stable, expressed as data in the rulebook itself, never as an ad-hoc application cache). Each row names the entity, the refresh contract, the refresh function that implements it, and the measured cost that justified materializing it. If this table's row for an entity were deleted, the entity's fields should still be regeneratable identically from the SSoT + the refresh function — that is the test for whether a materialization here is principled.

schema.name: MaterializedEntityId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. mat-sweep-study-summary).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label, mirrors MaterializedEntityId.

schema.name: EntityTable
schema.Description: The rulebook table whose fields are materialized (e.g. SweepStudySummary).

schema.name: RefreshContract
schema.Description: eager-on-write | on-demand | scheduled — when the refresh function is invoked relative to writes on the upstream data.

schema.name: RefreshFunction
schema.Description: The SQL function (in the customize-* override files) that recomputes this entity's materialized fields from its upstream SSoT tables.

schema.name: UpstreamTables
schema.Description: Comma-separated list of the tables this materialization is derived from — its sealed/stable upstream.

schema.name: Justification
schema.Description: The measured cost or evidence that justified materializing this entity instead of leaving it as a live calculated/aggregation field.

Methodology.Description: Table: Methodology — the first-principles process used to build this domain model. Each row documents one methodological axiom or step. Together these rows let a reader (or an LLM) reconstruct why the model is shaped the way it is, and re-apply the same process to a new domain.

schema.name: MethodologyId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. method-01-cmcc, method-02-dag-first).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label, mirrors MethodologyId.

schema.name: Phase
schema.Description: Broad phase: axiom | decomposition | derivation | hydration | validation | taxonomy | conclusion

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short title of the methodological principle or step.

schema.name: Statement
schema.Description: One-to-three sentence statement of the principle, precise enough to be applied or falsified.

schema.name: WhyItMatters
schema.Description: Why this step or principle is load-bearing for this model — what breaks if you skip it.

schema.name: IllustratedByLoop
schema.Description: LoopId(s) where this principle was concretely demonstrated (comma-separated).

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition that introduced or most relies on this methodological approach.

schema.name: PioneeringResearcher
schema.Description: FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher most associated with developing or popularising this methodological principle in the Simpson's Paradox context.

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionPrimaryVenue
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.PrimaryVenue via TraditionId.

schema.name: PioneeringResearcherName
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Name via PioneeringResearcher.

schema.name: PioneeringResearcherAffiliation
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Affiliation via PioneeringResearcher.

schema.name: LoopCount
schema.Description: Count of Loops whose TraditionId matches this methodology entry's TraditionId — how many build iterations this methodological tradition has driven.

Conclusions.Description: Table: Conclusions — the substantive epistemic findings that this model has produced or is designed to produce. Each row is one conclusion: a claim about the domain, about the instrument, or about the methodology that is either already witnessed or is the target of a planned loop.

schema.name: ConclusionId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. conc-01-paradox-is-derived).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label, mirrors ConclusionId.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: domain | instrument | methodology | open-question | corpus-theorem

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: witnessed | planned | open

schema.name: ReportTier
schema.Description: critical | supporting | transient — controls how much space this conclusion gets in the generated PDF/summary report. critical = full section with evidence + chart; supporting = 1-2 line bullet; transient = folded into a single compact list.

schema.name: ValidatingHypothesis
schema.Description: FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId. The pre-registered hypothesis whose live DiscoveryFindings.IsConfirmed value is the current-truth check for this conclusion — set on theorem/corpus-theorem conclusions so a report can distinguish a conclusion's archived prose (may say 'Proved') from its live status (may since be contradicted by corpus growth). Null when no single hypothesis tests this exact claim.

schema.name: LiveStatusConfirmed
schema.Description: Lookup: DiscoveryFindings.IsConfirmed via ValidatingHypothesis — the current pass/fail state of the hypothesis backing this conclusion, recomputed on every build from live corpus data. NULL when ValidatingHypothesis is not set (no live check exists for this conclusion).

schema.name: LiveStatusObservedMetric
schema.Description: Lookup: DiscoveryFindings.ObservedMetric via ValidatingHypothesis — the current observed values for the hypothesis backing this conclusion, so a reader can see exactly why it currently passes or fails.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: One-line statement of the conclusion.

schema.name: Evidence
schema.Description: The specific DB query result, loop outcome, or argument that establishes or will establish this conclusion.

schema.name: WitnessedInLoop
schema.Description: LoopId where this conclusion was first witnessed (if status=witnessed).

schema.name: TargetLoop
schema.Description: LoopId that is designed to witness this conclusion (if status=planned).

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The tradition whose theoretical framework this conclusion primarily vindicates, refines, or challenges.

schema.name: ResearcherId
schema.Description: FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. The researcher whose specific claim, definition, or framework this conclusion directly affirms.

schema.name: ChallengesResearcher
schema.Description: FK → Researchers.ResearcherId. A researcher whose position this conclusion refutes or qualifies (e.g. conc-03 refutes Blyth's unanimity criterion as the primary definition).

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionKeyClaimSummary
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.KeyClaim via TraditionId — shows which tradition's central claim this conclusion validates or refines.

schema.name: ResearcherName
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Name via ResearcherId.

schema.name: ResearcherAffiliation
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Affiliation via ResearcherId.

schema.name: ChallengesResearcherName
schema.Description: Lookup: Researchers.Name via ChallengesResearcher.

schema.name: WitnessedInLoopTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Loops.Title via WitnessedInLoop.

schema.name: WitnessedInLoopCommitHash
schema.Description: Lookup: Loops.CommitHash via WitnessedInLoop — git checkout this SHA to replay the instrument state when this conclusion was first witnessed.

schema.name: WitnessedInLoopCommitShort
schema.Description: Lookup: Loops.CommitShort via WitnessedInLoop.

schema.name: WitnessedInLoopCommitDate
schema.Description: Lookup: Loops.CommitDate via WitnessedInLoop.

schema.name: WitnessedInLoopGitTag
schema.Description: Lookup: Loops.GitTag via WitnessedInLoop — preferred replay entry point when a milestone tag exists.

schema.name: InvariantProtectingCount
schema.Description: Count of InvariantChecks whose ProtectsConclusion FK points to this conclusion — how many algebraic invariants stand behind this finding.

UIScreens.Description: Table: UIScreens — the canonical screens that comprise the Simpson's Paradox Explorer UI. Each row defines one screen: its purpose, its primary data source (which rulebook view it reads), and the user action it supports. A developer can reconstruct the full UI from this table without any prior knowledge of the application.

schema.name: ScreenId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. screen-01-study-list).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label, mirrors ScreenId.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Human-readable screen title shown in the UI.

schema.name: Route
schema.Description: URL path pattern (e.g. /, /study/:studyId, /compare).

schema.name: PrimaryEntity
schema.Description: The main rulebook entity this screen reads from (the vw_* view name without prefix).

schema.name: Purpose
schema.Description: One sentence: what a user can learn or do on this screen.

schema.name: KeyFields
schema.Description: Comma-separated list of the most important fields displayed on this screen.

schema.name: UserAction
schema.Description: Primary user action available on this screen (e.g. select a study, drill into a stratum).

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId. The scholarly tradition whose lens this screen primarily serves — determines which framing language and which fields are most prominent.

schema.name: PrimaryConclusion
schema.Description: FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion this screen most directly visualises or communicates to the user.

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionCoreConern
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.CoreConcern via TraditionId — the lens this screen applies.

schema.name: PrimaryConclusionTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Title via PrimaryConclusion.

schema.name: PrimaryConclusionEvidence
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Evidence via PrimaryConclusion.

UIComponents.Description: Table: UIComponents — the reusable visual building blocks used across UIScreens. Each row defines one component: what it renders, which entity it reads, and what props it requires. A developer can implement the full UI by composing these components onto the screens defined in UIScreens.

schema.name: ComponentId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. comp-01-distortion-badge).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label, mirrors ComponentId.

schema.name: ComponentName
schema.Description: Code-level component name (PascalCase, e.g. DistortionBadge).

schema.name: UsedOnScreens
schema.Description: Comma-separated ScreenIds where this component appears.

schema.name: PrimaryEntity
schema.Description: The rulebook entity this component reads (the vw_* view).

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What this component renders and what decisions a developer must make to implement it.

schema.name: RequiredFields
schema.Description: Comma-separated rulebook fields this component must receive as props.

schema.name: VisualSpec
schema.Description: Plain-English description of the visual output: shape, color coding, interactivity.

InstrumentSpec.Description: Table: InstrumentSpec — the complete specification of the Simpson's Paradox classification instrument. One row per component: required input fields, derived coordinates, classification outputs, and adapter requirements. Every row is machine-verifiable against the 238-study corpus. This table is the distillation of loops 1–25 into a reusable tool.

schema.name: SpecId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this instrument component.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ComponentType
schema.Description: input | derived | classification | adapter-requirement

schema.name: FieldName
schema.Description: The field or variable name in the DAG.

schema.name: DataType
schema.Description: integer | decimal | boolean | string | categorical

schema.name: SourceTable
schema.Description: The DAG table where this field is computed or stored.

schema.name: Formula
schema.Description: The ERB formula or derivation rule for this component.

schema.name: NaturalLanguage
schema.Description: Plain-English statement of what this component represents or requires.

schema.name: AdapterNote
schema.Description: What a researcher must supply or verify to satisfy this component when hydrating a new study.

AllocationSweep.Description: Table: AllocationSweep — parametric sweep of treatment-A allocation fraction within each study's most confounded stratum (see SweepStudyConfig). Ten fractions from 0.05 to 0.95 per study. SweepCorrectedGap is invariant; SweepPooledGap wanders.

schema.name: SweepId
schema.Description: Unique row identifier: <study>-f<pct> where pct is AllocFractionA*100.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: StudyId
schema.Description: The study being swept — all 238 studies in the corpus.

schema.name: AllocFractionA
schema.Description: Fraction of the sweep-stratum cases assigned to treatment A. Ranges from 0.05 to 0.95. The sweep stratum is the one whose allocation was most confounded in the original study.

schema.name: IsOriginal
schema.Description: TRUE for the row closest to the actual original study allocation.

schema.name: NSweepStratumTotal
schema.Description: Total cases in the sweep stratum (the stratum whose allocation is being varied). Fixed per study.

schema.name: NSweepA
schema.Description: Cases in the sweep stratum assigned to treatment A = ROUND(NSweepStratumTotal * AllocFractionA).

schema.name: NSweepB
schema.Description: Cases in the sweep stratum assigned to treatment B = NSweepStratumTotal - NSweepA.

schema.name: NFixedA
schema.Description: Total A-treatment cases in all NON-swept strata (held fixed across sweep rows). For kidney-1986 = 87 (small-stratum A). For berkeley-1973 = 1302 (depts B+C+D male). For compressed-synthetic = 80 (easy-stratum A). For balanced-synthetic = 100 (hard-stratum A).

schema.name: NFixedB
schema.Description: Total B-treatment cases in all NON-swept strata (held fixed across sweep rows). For kidney-1986 = 270 (small-stratum B). For berkeley-1973 = 993 (depts B+C+D female). For compressed-synthetic = 120 (easy-stratum B). For balanced-synthetic = 100 (hard-stratum B).

schema.name: SweepRateA
schema.Description: Per-cell success rate for treatment A in the sweep stratum. Fixed per study.

schema.name: SweepRateB
schema.Description: Per-cell success rate for treatment B in the sweep stratum. Fixed per study.

schema.name: FixedRateA
schema.Description: Weighted success rate for treatment A across all fixed (non-swept) strata. Constant per study.

schema.name: FixedRateB
schema.Description: Weighted success rate for treatment B across all fixed (non-swept) strata. Constant per study.

schema.name: SweepPooledRateA
schema.Description: Pooled success rate for treatment A = (NSweepA * SweepRateA + NFixedA * FixedRateA) / (NSweepA + NFixedA). Changes as AllocFractionA varies.

schema.name: SweepPooledRateB
schema.Description: Pooled success rate for treatment B = (NSweepB * SweepRateB + NFixedB * FixedRateB) / (NSweepB + NFixedB). Changes as AllocFractionA varies.

schema.name: SweepPooledGap
schema.Description: SweepPooledRateA minus SweepPooledRateB. Wanders as AllocFractionA varies because pooled rates mix stratum sizes unequally. Crosses zero at some allocation (the paradox reversal point).

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGap
schema.Description: Allocation-free signal: sum over strata of (n_s/N) * (rate_As - rate_Bs). Constant across the sweep by construction — the allocation variable does not appear. This is the conserved quantity under re-stratification.

schema.name: AllocationDistortionWitness
schema.Description: SweepPooledGap minus SweepCorrectedGap. The allocation distortion: how much the pooled signal has been inflated or deflated by differential stratum allocation. Zero only when allocation is balanced.

schema.name: SweepStudyId
schema.Description: FK to Studies.StudyId. One row per study in the AllocationSweep.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: DistortionTypeLabel
schema.Description: DistortionType for this study (A, B, C, or D) — provides context for what the sweep should show.

schema.name: CorrectedGapConstant
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary(), 03b/05b customize SQL). The allocation-free corrected gap for this study = MINIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepCorrectedGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). Upstream (AllocationSweep, SweepStudyConfig) is sealed/stable per build; recomputing this per-row via nested calc_* calls was measured to fan out to 22.5s for a 242-study corpus scan with zero memoization — materialized here instead of left as a live calculated field. See MaterializedEntities.

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGapMax
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Maximum SweepCorrectedGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MAXIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepCorrectedGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGapMin
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Minimum SweepCorrectedGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MINIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepCorrectedGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: SweepCorrectedGapRange
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepCorrectedGap across the sweep = SweepCorrectedGapMax - SweepCorrectedGapMin. Should be effectively zero (< 0.0001) for all studies if CorrectedGap is truly allocation-free. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: SweepPooledGapMax
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Maximum SweepPooledGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MAXIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepPooledGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: SweepPooledGapMin
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Minimum SweepPooledGap across all allocation fractions for this study = MINIFS(AllocationSweep[SweepPooledGap], AllocationSweep[StudyId], SweepStudyId). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: SweepPooledGapRange
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). Range of SweepPooledGap across the sweep = SweepPooledGapMax - SweepPooledGapMin. Should be large for Type A/B studies (pooled gap wanders) and smaller for Type D (balanced allocation = less wandering). See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: PooledGapCrossesZero
schema.Description: Principled materialization (refresh_sweep_study_summary()). TRUE when SweepPooledGapMin < 0 and SweepPooledGapMax > 0 — the pooled gap changes sign somewhere in the sweep, meaning the paradox can be created or destroyed by pure allocation. See CorrectedGapConstant for why this is materialized rather than calculated.

schema.name: InvariantWitness
schema.Description: Summary witness statement: PASS if CorrectedGap is invariant (range < 0.0001), FAIL otherwise.
schema.formula: =IF({{SweepCorrectedGapRange}} < 0.0001, "PASS: CorrectedGap invariant across allocation sweep", "FAIL: CorrectedGap varies — formula error")

schema.name: SweepStratumLabel
schema.Description: Which stratum is being swept for this study.

ResearchTraditions.Description: Table: ResearchTraditions — the six scholarly lineages that have engaged with Simpson's Paradox. Each row is a named intellectual tradition, not a specific paper or result.

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: Slug identifier, e.g. tradition-historical.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Short display name for the tradition.

schema.name: Era
schema.Description: Approximate active period, e.g. "1899–1972".

schema.name: CoreConcern
schema.Description: The central question this tradition asks about the paradox.

schema.name: PrimaryVenue
schema.Description: Where this tradition publishes (journal, conference, book series).

schema.name: KeyClaim
schema.Description: The tradition's signature thesis or resolution strategy.

schema.name: OpenQuestion
schema.Description: What this tradition leaves unresolved or contested.

schema.name: VerifiedByDeepResearch
schema.Description: True if the 2026-06-28 adversarial deep-research pass confirmed the tradition at high confidence.

schema.name: IllustratedByStudy
schema.Description: FK → Studies.StudyId. The study in this model that most vividly illustrates this tradition's characteristic concern or resolution strategy.

schema.name: SupportingConclusion
schema.Description: FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion in this model that most directly validates or advances this tradition's central claim about Simpson's Paradox.

schema.name: IllustratedByStudyTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Studies.Title via IllustratedByStudy.

schema.name: IllustratedByStudySource
schema.Description: Lookup: Studies.Source via IllustratedByStudy — the citation for the study that best illustrates this tradition.

schema.name: IllustratedByStudyDistortionType
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.DistortionType for the illustrated study.

schema.name: SupportingConclusionTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Title via SupportingConclusion.

schema.name: SupportingConclusionCategory
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Category via SupportingConclusion.

schema.name: ResearcherCount
schema.Description: Count of researchers in this tradition.

schema.name: StudyCount
schema.Description: Count of studies in this model whose TraditionId matches this tradition.

schema.name: LoopCount
schema.Description: Count of Leopold Loops that drew primarily on this tradition's conceptual vocabulary.

Researchers.Description: Table: Researchers — individuals who have made named, verified contributions to understanding Simpson's Paradox. Linked to ResearchTraditions by TraditionId.

schema.name: ResearcherId
schema.Description: Slug, e.g. researcher-pearson.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Full name as commonly cited.

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId

schema.name: ActivePeriod
schema.Description: Approximate years of relevant work, e.g. "1899".

schema.name: Affiliation
schema.Description: Primary institutional affiliation for the relevant work.

schema.name: KeyContribution
schema.Description: One-sentence description of their specific contribution to the paradox literature.

schema.name: SignatureWork
schema.Description: The canonical paper or book most associated with their contribution.

schema.name: RelationToHannanPearl
schema.Description: How this researcher's line of work relates to or differs from Hannan (aggregation bias) and Pearl (DAG/do-calculus).

schema.name: VerifiedByDeepResearch
schema.Description: True if the 2026-06-28 adversarial deep-research pass confirmed this person at high confidence.

schema.name: ConfidenceLevel
schema.Description: high | medium | unverified — matches deep-research vote outcome.

schema.name: CanonicalStudyId
schema.Description: FK → Studies.StudyId. The study in this model that best illustrates this researcher's specific contribution to the Simpson's Paradox literature.

schema.name: IllustratesConclusion
schema.Description: FK → Conclusions.ConclusionId. The conclusion in this model that this researcher's work directly supports, challenges, or frames.

schema.name: TraditionName
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Name via TraditionId.

schema.name: TraditionEra
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.Era via TraditionId.

schema.name: CanonicalStudyTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Studies.Title via CanonicalStudyId.

schema.name: CanonicalStudyDistortionType
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.DistortionType for the canonical study — the geometric type of paradox this researcher's work exemplifies.

schema.name: CanonicalStudyPolicyImplication
schema.Description: Lookup: TreatmentRankings.CorrectedPolicyImplication for the canonical study.

schema.name: IllustratesConclusionTitle
schema.Description: Lookup: Conclusions.Title via IllustratesConclusion.

schema.name: TraditionKeyClaimSummary
schema.Description: Lookup: ResearchTraditions.KeyClaim via TraditionId — the tradition's signature thesis this researcher contributed to.

SyntheticPhase.Description: Table: SyntheticPhase — a parametric grid of canonical 2×2 synthetic studies at controlled (ParamStratumFraction, ParamStratumGap1, ParamStratumGap2, ParamAllocationBias) coordinates. Stratum 1 is the hard stratum (base rate 0.45); stratum 2 is easy (base rate 0.75). Each row constructs cell counts from the parameters and derives PhaseDistortionType using the same geometric classification as TreatmentRankings. This maps the A/B/C+/C-/D phase boundaries algebraically — independent of empirical corpus data.

schema.name: PhaseId
schema.Description: Unique grid-point identifier encoding (w, g1, g2, b) coordinates.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ParamStratumFraction
schema.Description: w — fraction of total cases in the hard (stratum-1) subgroup. Range 0.30–0.75 in this grid.

schema.name: ParamStratumGap1
schema.Description: g1 — signed treatment advantage (rate_A − rate_B) in the hard stratum. Positive means A wins stratum 1.

schema.name: ParamStratumGap2
schema.Description: g2 — signed treatment advantage in the easy stratum. Negative g2 enables type-B (partial reversal) cells.

schema.name: ParamAllocationBias
schema.Description: b — allocation bias in stratum 1: extra fraction of stratum-1 cases assigned to treatment A above 50/50. Range 0.00–0.95.

schema.name: PhaseS1Total
schema.Description: Total cases in stratum 1 = ROUND(200 × ParamStratumFraction).

schema.name: PhaseNA1
schema.Description: Treatment-A cases in stratum 1.

schema.name: PhaseNB1
schema.Description: Treatment-B cases in stratum 1.

schema.name: PhaseNA2
schema.Description: Treatment-A cases in stratum 2.

schema.name: PhaseNB2
schema.Description: Treatment-B cases in stratum 2.

schema.name: PhaseRateA1
schema.Description: Success rate for A in hard stratum.

schema.name: PhaseRateB1
schema.Description: Success rate for B in hard stratum.

schema.name: PhaseRateA2
schema.Description: Success rate for A in easy stratum.

schema.name: PhaseRateB2
schema.Description: Success rate for B in easy stratum.

schema.name: PhasePooledRateA
schema.Description: Pooled success rate for treatment A.

schema.name: PhasePooledRateB
schema.Description: Pooled success rate for treatment B.

schema.name: PhaseSignedPooledGap
schema.Description: Pooled rate gap (A minus B).

schema.name: PhaseCorrectedGap
schema.Description: Allocation-free equal-weight gap = w×g1 + (1−w)×g2.

schema.name: PhaseStrataWonByLoser
schema.Description: Count of strata whose per-stratum winner disagrees with the pooled winner.

schema.name: PhaseReversalIntensity
schema.Description: PhaseStrataWonByLoser / 2 for this 2-stratum template.

schema.name: PhaseIsSignFlip
schema.Description: TRUE when equal-weight and pooled gaps have opposite signs.

schema.name: PhaseAllocationDistortion
schema.Description: |PhaseCorrectedGap − PhaseSignedPooledGap|.

schema.name: PhaseDistortionType
schema.Description: Five-type geometric classification at this parameter point.
schema.formula: =IF(AND({{PhaseIsSignFlip}}, {{PhaseReversalIntensity}} = 1), "A", IF(AND({{PhaseIsSignFlip}}, {{PhaseReversalIntensity}} < 1), "B", IF(AND(NOT({{PhaseIsSignFlip}}), {{PhaseAllocationDistortion}} > 0.01, ABS({{PhaseSignedPooledGap}}) > ABS({{PhaseCorrectedGap}}) + 0.001), "C+", IF(AND(NOT({{PhaseIsSignFlip}}), {{PhaseAllocationDistortion}} > 0.01, ABS({{PhaseSignedPooledGap}}) < ABS({{PhaseCorrectedGap}}) - 0.001), "C-", "D"))))

PhaseDiagramSummary.Description: Table: PhaseDiagramSummary — one row summarising the SyntheticPhase grid. Witnesses that all five DistortionType cells (A, B, C+, C-, D) are nonempty in parameter space.

schema.name: PhaseDiagramId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this phase diagram instance.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: PhasePointCount
schema.Description: Total grid points in SyntheticPhase (sum of type counts). Refreshed from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: PhaseTypeACount
schema.Description: Grid points classified as type A. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: PhaseTypeBCount
schema.Description: Grid points classified as type B. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: PhaseTypeCPlusCount
schema.Description: Grid points classified as type C+. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: PhaseTypeCMinusCount
schema.Description: Grid points classified as type C-. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: PhaseTypeDCount
schema.Description: Grid points classified as type D. Populated from vw_synthetic_phase by 05b-customize-data.sql.

schema.name: AllFiveTypesPresent
schema.Description: TRUE when every DistortionType cell has at least one grid point.

schema.name: PhaseTaxonomyWitness
schema.Description: Human-readable witness string of type counts across the grid.

schema.name: PhaseWitnessNote
schema.Description: PASS/FAIL summary for taxonomy completeness.
schema.formula: =IF({{AllFiveTypesPresent}}, "PASS: all five distortion types populated in parameter space", "FAIL: missing distortion type in grid")

IngestionProtocol.Description: Table: IngestionProtocol — the minimal formal contract for adding a new 2×K study to the corpus.

schema.name: ProtocolId
schema.Description: Unique slug for this contract item.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: RuleType
schema.Description: required-input | structural | inclusion | exclusion | adapter-metadata

schema.name: TargetTable
schema.Description: Rulebook table this item applies to.

schema.name: FieldName
schema.Description: Column name when RuleType=required-input.

schema.name: RequirementStatement
schema.Description: Plain-English statement of the requirement.

schema.name: MechanicalCheck
schema.Description: How to verify compliance before classification.

schema.name: SortOrder
schema.Description: Display order in the ingestion checklist.

IngestionSummary.Description: Table: IngestionSummary — witnesses that the current corpus satisfies the IngestionProtocol contract.

schema.name: IngestionSummaryId
schema.Description: Unique identifier.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ProtocolItemCount
schema.Description: Total contract items. Fixed loop-90: COUNTIFS(...,"<>") mistranslates to a literal '<>' string-equality filter (a known transpiler bug) instead of IS NOT NULL, always returning 0; replaced with bare COUNT(), which transpiles correctly.

schema.name: CorpusCellCount
schema.Description: Total CaseCell rows. Fixed loop-90 (see ProtocolItemCount).

schema.name: ValidCellCount
schema.Description: Valid CaseCells.

schema.name: AllCellsValid
schema.Description: Every cell passes validity predicate.

schema.name: StudyCount
schema.Description: Count of studies subject to the ingestion contract (excludes control studies, loop-92 -- control studies were never encoded with paired treatment arms, so IngestionCellParity is structurally undefined for them, not a real ingestion defect).

schema.name: StructuralCompliantCount
schema.Description: Studies with IngestionCellParity.

schema.name: AllStudiesStructural
schema.Description: All studies structurally valid.

schema.name: RealStudyCount

CandidateStudyCatalog.Description: Table: CandidateStudyCatalog — curated backlog of published Simpson's-paradox-eligible studies. Tracks import status, expected distortion type, and encoding metadata before CaseCells exist.

schema.Description: Unique slug for this catalog entry.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.Description: Human-readable study title.

schema.Description: Primary publication citation.

schema.Description: Pre-registered stratifying variable name for encoding.

schema.name: ObservedSignFlipType

schema.Description: not_started | downloaded | manual_only | blocked

schema.name: IsDataReady
schema.Description: TRUE when stratified data is downloaded or available from published tables.

schema.name: CatalogSummaryId
schema.Description: Unique identifier.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.Description: Candidates with Priority <= 2.

ConfounderIdentities.Description: Table: ConfounderIdentities — canonical cross-study archetypes for stratum variables. Each identity names a reusable confounding mechanism accountable across the corpus (loop-80).

schema.name: ConfounderIdentityId

StratumVariableIdentityMaps.Description: Table: StratumVariableIdentityMaps — many-to-one map from per-study StratumVariables to canonical ConfounderIdentities (loop-80).

schema.name: VariableNameNormalized

IdentityClusterSummaries.Description: Table: IdentityClusterSummaries — witnessed geometry rollup per ConfounderIdentity (manifest flip, latent-D, avg distortion) — loop-80 open-question substrate.

schema.name: IdentityClusterId

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

ConfounderDistortionTimeline.Description: Table: ConfounderDistortionTimeline -- derived geometry at the (ConfounderIdentity x ValidTimeDecade) grain. Each row aggregates the studies whose real-world cohort period (valid-time, not publication year) falls in that decade and that carry the given confounder archetype, tracking whether the corpus drifts toward clean Type-D (non-reversing, zero-allocation-distortion) outcomes over time and whether that drift is confounder-specific. Principled materialization per CLAUDE.md (N-way join over a sealed, bounded study set) -- refreshed via refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline(), same pattern as IdentityClusterSummaries/IdentityDomainCells.

schema.name: CellId
schema.Description: Unique slug: confounder-identity-id + '-x-' + valid-time decade (e.g. id-disease-severity-x-1980).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ConfounderIdentity
schema.Description: FK -> ConfounderIdentities.

schema.name: ValidTimeDecade
schema.Description: Studies.ValidTimeDecade bucket this cell aggregates over (e.g. 1980 covers valid-time midpoints 1980-1989).

schema.name: StudyCount
schema.Description: Studies carrying this ConfounderIdentity whose ValidTimeDecade falls in this bucket (refresh_confounder_distortion_timeline()).

schema.name: TypeDCount
schema.Description: Of those, count with TreatmentRankings.DistortionType = 'D' (clean, non-reversing).

schema.name: SignFlipCount
schema.Description: Of those, count with TreatmentRankings.IsSignFlip = TRUE.

schema.name: SumSignalPurity
schema.Description: Sum of TreatmentRankings.SignalPurity across cell studies (numerator for MeanSignalPurity; summed rather than pre-averaged so the aggregate survives refresh re-runs without drift).

schema.name: SumAllocationDistortion
schema.Description: Sum of TreatmentRankings.AllocationDistortion across cell studies (numerator for MeanAllocationDistortion).

schema.name: TypeDFraction
schema.Description: TypeDCount / StudyCount.

schema.name: SignFlipFraction
schema.Description: SignFlipCount / StudyCount.

schema.name: MeanSignalPurity
schema.Description: SumSignalPurity / StudyCount.

schema.name: MeanAllocationDistortion
schema.Description: SumAllocationDistortion / StudyCount.

schema.name: IdentityDisplayName
schema.Description: Lookup: ConfounderIdentities.DisplayName via ConfounderIdentity.

schema.name: MechanismClass
schema.Description: Lookup: ConfounderIdentities.MechanismClass via ConfounderIdentity — the archetype group used to test whether Type-D drift is confounder-specific.

schema.name: IdentityEarliestDecade
schema.Description: Earliest ValidTimeDecade present in this table for this ConfounderIdentity.

schema.name: IdentityLatestDecade
schema.Description: Latest ValidTimeDecade present in this table for this ConfounderIdentity.

schema.name: IdentityEarliestTypeDFraction
schema.Description: TypeDFraction of this ConfounderIdentity's earliest decade cell -- the start point for the drift comparison.

schema.name: IdentityLatestTypeDFraction
schema.Description: TypeDFraction of this ConfounderIdentity's latest decade cell -- the end point for the drift comparison.

schema.name: DriftDirection
schema.Description: rising | falling | flat | single-decade -- compares this ConfounderIdentity's earliest-decade TypeDFraction to its latest-decade TypeDFraction across the whole table (same value on every row for a given ConfounderIdentity by construction).

schema.name: BalanceId
schema.Description: Primary key (singleton: 'corpus-balance')

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: =BalanceId

schema.name: TotalStudyCount
schema.Description: Total studies in corpus (Simpson + control). Fixed loop-90: was authored with COUNTA/bareword refs and an uppercase Formula key, which the transpiler cannot parse. COUNTIFS(...,"<>") also mistranslates (known transpiler bug: literal '<>' string instead of IS NOT NULL), so this is expressed as SimpsonStudyCount + ControlStudyCount instead.

schema.name: SimpsonStudyCount
schema.Description: Studies where IsControlStudy=FALSE. Fixed loop-90 (see TotalStudyCount).

schema.name: ControlStudyCount
schema.Description: Studies where IsControlStudy=TRUE. Fixed loop-90 (see TotalStudyCount).

schema.name: ControlFraction
schema.Description: ControlStudyCount / TotalStudyCount. Fixed loop-90 (see TotalStudyCount); returns 0 for the degenerate empty-corpus edge instead of "" (see TreatmentRankings.DistortionRatio for why IF(x=0,"",...) is unsafe on a numeric-typed field).
